source_pr.title: util/types: fix order by binary
source_pr.body: In Orderby Clause, as using BINARY operator to cast the following operand to a binary string, here meet an error. Improved IsOrderedType() to handle it.

symptoms: mustExecMatch(c, se, "select * from t order by 1", [][]interface{}{{1, 2, []byte("abc")}, {2, 1, []byte("bcd")}}) (session_test.go)
failure_signature: mustExecMatch(c, se, "select * from t order by 1", [][]interface{}{{1, 2, []byte("abc")}, {2, 1, []byte("bcd")}}) (session_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it relied on nondeterministic result ordering (e.g., missing ORDER BY / unsorted assertions). Result ordering is not guaranteed unless explicitly enforced. This PR stabilizes it by: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.
fix_pattern: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.

evidence: evidence: mustExecMatch(c, se, "select * from t order by 1", [][]interface{}{{1, 2, []byte("abc")}, {2, 1, []byte("bcd")}}) (session_test.go)
